title: My AI coding assistant stack in 2025
post:
After months of testing every AI coding tool out there, I've finally settled on my 2025 stack — and it's smaller than I expected.

Cursor handles my main editing workflow. Claude Max takes care of the big architectural decisions. And a small custom script ties them together so I stop context-switching.

The biggest surprise? The old-school tools still matter. My terminal setup and keyboard shortcuts save me more time than any AI feature.

What's your AI coding stack look like in 2025? Thoughts?
